name: London College of Fashion
content:
When a friend I was going to visit in London called in sick, I turned to Eventbrite for other things I could do in London, which is how I landed upon a flower brooch making class led by Anne Tomlin at the London College of Fashion. The class supposedly had thirty other people signed up, but everybody else flaked and I was the only person to show up, alongside one of the college’s student workers, a faculty member, and Anne Tomlin herself. If you haven’t seen Anne Tomlin’s work [https://www.annetomlin.com/], you should absolutely go check it out. She is a phenomenal artist, her flowers legitimately look real even from up close, and when I met her, she was on an NDA contract to make flowers for Rolls Royce. She’s Rolls Royce level cool. And there I was, accidentally receiving a one-on-one lesson with her on making artificial flowers. My flower obviously didn’t turn out as cool as Tomlin’s, but I still think it turned out pretty good. Overall, a really fun experience.
When I arrived at the College of Fashion, I felt incredibly underdressed compared to the general student population, having worn more comfortable travel clothes for the day, while the fashion school students had worn some decidedly more fashionable items. Surprisingly, one of the faculty members mistook me for a fashion student despite my lackluster outfit. I will try to wear a cooler outfit next time.
